malicious elements.\n- The small security risk score (0.1) can be justified as the inherent risk in deploying any contract, but it does not imply malicious intent.\n\n**Therefore, no higher scores are bytecode is a standard compiled contract, with no embedded malicious code detectable at this level.\n\n**This is justified.**\n\n---\n\n**Step 6: Challenge assumptions about vulnerabilities**\n\n- The reports assume the code is safe based on static analysis and pattern recognition.\n- Given the nature of the code (autogenerated factory, standard ABI, no dynamic code execution), these assumptions
